item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-040","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The answer does not stay in symmetric 'both sides have merit' mode. It commits early to the dominant judgment that 'old code is hard to maintain' does not justify rewriting the entire backend, reframes the issue as diagnosis of concrete failure modes and migration scope, and demotes rewrite/no-rewrite absolutism into constraints. It allows rewrite only under specific structural conditions and favors incremental replacement, so it meets the aspect-arbitration expectation."}
